fab_section_md:
- Prince Vaegon, born 63 AC, was quiet, sour, and unlike his elder brothers, preferring books to the yard. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:23)
- He publicly refused to wed his sister Daella, mocking her as unable to read. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:111)
- Grand Maester Elysar judged he had the makings of an archmaester, and he was sent to the Citadel. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:163)
identity_line: Vaegon — Prince Vaegon, born 63 AC, was quiet, sour, and unlike his elder brothers, preferring books to the yard.

fab_section_md:
- Lady Jocelyn Baratheon of Storm's End, one of the great beauties of the realm, wed Aemon in 70 AC. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:45)
- She bore Rhaenys, Jaehaerys and Alysanne's first grandchild, in 74 AC. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:145)
- She was daughter of Rogar Baratheon and Queen Alyssa, half-sister to Jaehaerys. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:181)
identity_line: Jocelyn Baratheon — Lady Jocelyn Baratheon of Storm's End, one of the great beauties of the realm, wed Aemon in 70 AC.

fab_section_md:
- Ser Lucamore Strong of the Kingsguard was found to have secretly taken three wives and fathered sixteen children. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:71)
- Discovered by Ser Ryam Redwyne, he confessed before the Iron Throne and was gelded and sent to the Wall. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:71)
- The smallfolk mocked him as "Ser Lucamore the Lusty." (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:73)
identity_line: Lucamore Strong — Ser Lucamore Strong of the Kingsguard was found to have secretly taken three wives and fathered sixteen children.

fab_section_md:
- Royce Blackwood, son of Lord Blackwood of Raventree, courted Daella at fifteen as a bowman, swordsman, and singer. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:213)
- The match failed because the Blackwoods kept the old gods. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:213)
identity_line: Royce Blackwood, son of Lord Blackwood of Raventree, courted Daella at fifteen as a bowman, swordsman, and singer.

fab_section_md:
- Boremund Baratheon, Lord of Storm's End at eight-and-twenty, was the most imposing of Daella's three suitors. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:235)
- As son of Lord Rogar by Queen Alyssa, he was half-brother to Jaehaerys and Alysanne. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:235)
identity_line: Boremund Baratheon, Lord of Storm's End at eight-and-twenty, was the most imposing of Daella's three suitors.

fab_section_md: - Ser Tymond Lannister, heir to Casterly Rock at twenty, was the wealthiest and one of the handsomest of Daella's suitors. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:239)
identity_line: Tymond Lannister — Ser Tymond Lannister, heir to Casterly Rock at twenty, was the wealthiest and one of the handsomest of Daella's suitors.

fab_section_md: - Princess Maegelle joined the Faith as a novice in 73 AC at ten years of age, a quiet, studious girl. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:61)
identity_line: Maegelle — Princess Maegelle joined the Faith as a novice in 73 AC at ten years of age, a quiet, studious girl.

fab_section_md: - Grand Maester Elysar counseled Jaehaerys and Alysanne on Vaegon and later nursed the infant Gael through her first year. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:163)
identity_line: Elysar — Grand Maester Elysar counseled Jaehaerys and Alysanne on Vaegon and later nursed the infant Gael through her first year.

fab_section_md: - Alaric Stark, Lord of Winterfell, died in 72 AC; his grandson Edric succeeded him. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:49)
identity_line: Alaric Stark, Lord of Winterfell, died in 72 AC; his grandson Edric succeeded him.
